@Situation:	Helen is upstairs in bed. Her father goes upstairs after her.

@Situation:	Her father asks whose little girl she is.

@Situation:	Her father asks if she would like to hear Santa Claus story.

@Situation:	Suddenly notices a cut on her father's chin which he received in shaving.

@Situation:	Her father asks if she will be dressed.

@Situation:	Her grandmother , as she is dressing Margaret , asks if her little sister Margaret wakened her.

@Situation:	Margaret on the floor.

@Situation:	Helen is dressed now and ready for breakfast.

@Situation:	Her father asks if it is too hot.

@Situation:	Coming out with her little red rocking chair.

@Situation:	Hellen is asked "What else do you want for breakfast?"

@Situation:	Her Grandmother gets her red chair.

@Situation:	Hellen is Speaking to Margaret.

@Situation:	Puts Vaseline on finger and rubs Margaret's nose

@Situation:	Helen sings

@Situation:	Takes Lucy's hands and makes her play on piano.

@Situation:	Stands on piano.

@Situation:	Her mother talks about slang to her daughter.

@Situation:	Helen is asked , "What do you say?"

@Situation:	Her Grandmother takes off bib.

@Situation:	Her mother speaks again about saying "Gee."

@Situation:	Her Grandmother rubs her neck.

@Situation:	Nancy tells Helen the story of a kitten.

@Situation:	Helen's Grandmother makes pretty yarn stitches on paper for Helen.

@Situation:	After Grandmother shows her how , she says

@Situation:	Her Grandmother talks of telling a story.

@Situation:	Grandmothers asks if she may borrow scissors to cut the thread.

@Situation:	Helen makes a dog with darning needle. Her mother talks to Helen.

@Situation:	Margaret is asleep on one end of the cot. Helen covers her dolly up , talking low to herself.

@Situation:	Grandmother speaks of books and asks Helen if she can tell her a story.

@Situation:	Here her Grandmother read stories that her mother might have a little rest from writing.

@Situation:	Maggie was a young woman living next door. She had come in for a few moments and had taken the pencil and paper to relieve the mother.

@Situation:	Dinner is ready.

@Situation:	Grandmother asks if she has done with her teeth.

@Situation:	Helen is asked , Would you like to go to high school? What would you do there?

@Situation:	Helen has been undressed preparatory for midday sleep.

@Situation:	Wakened up from nap.

@Situation:	Seeing shoes warming.

@Situation:	Helen , sitting at sewing machine.

@Situation:	Helen got burned at the oven.

@Situation:	Her father comes in.

@Situation:	Her father talks about his day at school.

@Situation:	Around the dining room walls the author had tacked a large sheet of paper for each letter in the alphabet and , as Helen had learned new words , they had been recorded on those sheets of paper.

@Situation:	In high chair.

@Situation:	Grandmother says "Margaret's a little midget."

@Situation:	Ten minutes , silent while busy eating.

@Situation:	Up on a couch with her father.
